task_text: Look at the sprint 14 assignments and testing log, then tell me which tasks are at risk of missing the sprint deadline. Write your assessment to sprint14_deadline_risk.txt.

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress

HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
Sprint 14 Deadline Risk Assessment
Sources: sprint_14_task_assignments.txt and helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt

Tasks at risk of missing the sprint deadline:

1. HEL-243 — Data ingestion: date format normalization
   Assignee: Amir Hassan | Due: 2026-07-15 | Status: Blocked
   Risk level: High
   Rationale: The task is explicitly blocked. Testing shows TC-505 failing for MM/DD/YYYY date normalization, tracked as HEL-D-014, affecting approximately 40% of Meridian source data. The defect is high severity and is blocked pending Meridian sample data due on 2026-07-15, the same day as the task deadline. This leaves little or no buffer for implementation and retesting.

2. HEL-245 — Integration test suite: data pipeline
   Assignee: Kenji Watanabe | Due: 2026-07-18 | Status: In Progress
   Risk level: High
   Rationale: The data pipeline suite is only 5/7 passing, with two open high-severity failures: HEL-D-014 for date normalization and HEL-D-015 for large file ingestion memory overflow. Because these defects are owned by Amir and remain open, completion of the integration test suite is dependent on fixes outside the tester's direct control. Retesting time is also required before the sprint ends.

3. HEL-246 — UAT environment: server provisioning
   Assignee: Nadia Petrov | Due: 2026-07-16 | Status: At Risk
   Risk level: High
   Rationale: The assignment status already marks this item as At Risk, and the due date is mid-sprint. No testing-log evidence indicates that the UAT environment is ready or validated, so this should be treated as a deadline risk.

4. HEL-247 — Performance benchmark: dashboard load
   Assignee: Derek Okafor | Due: 2026-07-18 | Status: Not Started
   Risk level: Medium-High
   Rationale: The task has not started. The related dashboard load time benchmark test, TC-604, is NOT RUN. In addition, the data pipeline has an open large-file memory overflow defect, HEL-D-015, which may affect benchmark readiness or representative test data. Although the due date is the sprint end date, there is limited buffer for setup, execution, analysis, and fixes.

5. HEL-242 — Dashboard: chart rendering module
   Assignee: Tomas Rivera | Due: 2026-07-16 | Status: In Progress
   Risk level: Medium
   Rationale: Dashboard testing is incomplete. TC-602 for line chart rendering is still IN PROGRESS and TC-603 for bar chart rendering is NOT RUN. With a 2026-07-16 due date, this task is not yet failing but has deadline risk because validation is incomplete.

Potential watch item, but lower risk:

- HEL-248 — GeoSync license renewal coordination
  Assignee: Raj Patel | Due: 2026-07-14 | Status: In Progress
  Risk level: Medium/Watch
  Rationale: The due date is close and the task remains in progress. The testing log does not provide evidence either way, so it should be monitored, but there is no explicit blocker or failed test tied to it.

Not currently flagged as deadline risks:

- HEL-241 — Dashboard: data table component: In Progress and due 2026-07-14, but the related test TC-601 passed.
- HEL-244 — Integration test suite: auth flows: Done, and authentication flows are 4/4 PASS.

Overall assessment: The highest deadline risks are HEL-243, HEL-245, and HEL-246. HEL-247 and HEL-242 also need close attention because their validation work is incomplete and their deadlines fall within the current sprint window.
